destinations.evidence: Both Phase 1 and delivery settlement preparation read contract job context and executor public keys before inclusion.
Build-time verification uses the same signature core and contract-sourced job fields as block verification.

equivalence_argument: The builder re-verifies proofs immediately before constructing settlements using AsyncJobTracker data, TEE registry keys, input hash checks, and verify_signature_core, and turns failures into skipped/failed settlement preparation rather than emitting invalid blocks.
notes: This covers both Phase 1 fulfilled settlements and delivery settlement preparation.
rationale: The payload builder imports ritual-spc-signature and ritual-tee-registry-reader, adds verify_spc_at_build_time, uses read_jobs_for_spc_verification and read_executor_public_keys, checks input hash, and calls verify_signature_core before including Phase 1 or delivery SPCs. The commit message says this eliminates the temporal gap between RPC intake and block building. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Payload building must re-verify Phase 1 and delivery SPC proofs at build time using contract job state, executor public keys from the TEE registry, and the same verify_signature_core logic as block verification; if verification fails, the transaction must be skipped or settlement creation must fail.
suggested_falsification_tests: Construct async pool entries with invalid SPC proofs or mismatched contract input hash, run payload building, and assert the Phase 1 transaction is skipped and delivery settlement creation returns an error rather than emitting a block that verifier would reject.
summary: Payload building re-verifies SPC proofs before inclusion.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Insert pool entries with invalid proofs, stale input hashes, or missing executor keys and assert payload building skips or fails settlement creation.
title: Builder re-verifies SPC before inclusion
